# monster-resident

A resident **MCP server** exposing **UCAN-verified orchestration** to local
fat-client stations. Part of the monster family; built on the Rust
[`a0n-auth`](../../auth/rust) UCAN crate and the
[`monster-guard`](../monster-guard) enforcement path.

- **What:** a stdio JSON-RPC 2.0 MCP server. Stations call the `orchestrate`
  tool with a capability token; the server verifies it and runs the delegated
  command under sandbox + lease.
- **Why:** local stations should get orchestration they can't abuse — every call
  is authorized by a UCAN rooted at this resident, and executed inside the
  monster-guard sandbox/lease (revocable mid-run).
- **How:** `serve` over stdio; provision stations with `grant`. Verification is
  `a0n_auth::verify_chain` (signatures + attenuation + revocation) plus a
  capability check (`orchestrate/run` on `plugin:<name>`).
- **Verified:** the `subagent_*` path is real. The
  [`monster-swarm`](../monster-swarm) Qwen quality gate spawns a real
  `fat-station` subagent through this stack and asserts the mesh predicts
  ` Paris` (token 12095) for "The capital of France is" — the real-inference
  ratchet. See the [`MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK`](../MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK.md).
- **Next:** bind tokens to a station DID + per-request invocation signatures
  (currently bearer); add a `run_topology` tool for monster GG/`.gnot`
  orchestration. The distributed-inference guardrails (UCAN-gated, cost-admitted
  CRUD over a leased subagent swarm) live in [`monster-swarm`](../monster-swarm)
  and are now surfaced here as the `subagent_*` + `swarm_*` MCP tools, with the
  `audit_*` tools surfacing its tamper-evident audit chain.

## Run

```
monster-resident                 # serve MCP over stdio
monster-resident whoami          # print the resident root DID
monster-resident grant --audience <did> --with plugin:<name> [--can orchestrate/run] [--caps fs,net] [--ttl <secs>]
```

`grant` mints a root-signed station capability (token to stdout). State (root
key, revocations) is shared with monster-guard under `$MONSTER_GUARD_HOME`
(default `~/.moonshine.d`).

## MCP tools

| Tool | Args | Effect |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `whoami` | — | the resident root DID (must root every capability) |
| `orchestrate` | `{ ucan, name, args? }` | verify the UCAN, then run `.bin/<name>` (monster-guard sandbox + lease) and return its output |
| `subagent_create` | `{ ucan, node, id?, caste?, caps?, lease_secs?, max_secs?, grant_ttl_secs?, node_args? }` | spawn a real distributed-inference node as a leased subagent (UCAN `agent/spawn`, cost-admitted) |
| `subagent_list` | — | list the live subagent fleet (local read) |
| `subagent_update` | `{ ucan, id, caps?, lease_secs?, grant_ttl_secs? }` | renew / re-attenuate a subagent's grant (UCAN `agent/update`) |
| `subagent_reap` | `{ ucan, id, cascade? }` | revoke a subagent (UCAN `agent/reap`); two-queen floor protects the last breeder. `cascade=true` reaps the agent **and its entire spawned subtree** (recursive closure over the `spawned_by` lineage graph) via `monster-swarm reap --cascade --json`, returning `{ reaped, count }` and naming the reaped subtree; each cascaded reap is a signed Reap entry in the [tamper-evident audit chain](../monster-swarm/README.md#cascade-revocation-containment). Default false (single-agent reap) |
| `swarm_vibe` | `{ dwell_ms? }` | read the partyline; return the Thoth `thoth.fleet.swarm.v1` frame |
| `swarm_autotick` | `{ ucan, node?, bud_at?, reap_below?, min_live?, caps?, lease_secs?, dwell_ms? }` | one autonomic homeostasis tick: bud a scout when collective consciousness is hot, reap an idle scout when calm (bounded by the UCAN + FRF ceiling) |
| `swarm_broadcast` | `{ consciousness, energy?, recalibration?, source_id?, count? }` | drive the partyline: emit SwarmSignal(s) of a given consciousness (0-5) to exercise/observe the live [autonomic re-fork](../monster-swarm/README.md#autonomic-re-fork-live) loop (`monster-swarm broadcast --json`); returns `{ emitted, consciousness, source_id }`. Broadcast hot (>=4) to provoke a bud, calm (0) to provoke a vent/reap |
| `audit_verify` | — | walk the [tamper-evident audit chain](../monster-swarm/README.md#tamper-evident-audit-chain) (`monster-swarm audit --verify --json`); returns `{ ok, entries, first_bad, reason, head }` plus the exit-code verdict (ok vs tamper) in the result text |
| `audit_log` | `{ limit? }` | read the audit chain (`monster-swarm audit --json`); returns the `{ entries }` of hash-chained, root-signed lifecycle events, optionally tailing the last `limit` |

The `audit_*` and `swarm_broadcast` tools, and `subagent_reap` when called with
`cascade=true`, shell out to the `monster-swarm` binary as a subprocess and
isolate the child's stderr from this server's MCP stdout stream — the child's
stdout is the JSON returned, its stderr never reaches the JSON-RPC transport.
(Plain `subagent_reap` without `cascade` keeps the in-process single-agent reap.)
The binary is located via `$MONSTER_SWARM_BIN`, the sibling crate's build output,
then PATH.

`orchestrate` rejects (`isError`) any token that fails verification, is revoked,
isn't rooted at this resident, or doesn't grant `orchestrate/run` on
`plugin:<name>`. The `subagent_*` tools are the [`monster-swarm`](../monster-swarm)
guardrails surfaced as MCP: each verifies `agent/<verb>` on `agent:fleet`
(`agent/*` grants all) rooted here, and is cost-admitted by the AntColony FRF
budget before monster-guard enforces the lease.

## Build

```
cargo build --release
```

## Example (manual)

```
export MONSTER_GUARD_HOME=/path/to/open-source/.moonshine.d
TOKEN=$(monster-resident grant --audience did:key:zStation --with plugin:bw --caps fs)
# then send JSON-RPC over stdin:
#   {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}}}
#   {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"orchestrate","arguments":{"ucan":"<TOKEN>","name":"bw","args":["grep","ACGT","data.fasta"]}}}
```
